(known as the **Argus Insurance Premier Division** for sponsorship reasons) was the 117th season of the national [amateur](/wiki/Amateur "Amateur") and [semi-professional](/wiki/Semi-professional "Semi-professional") [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") league in [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar") since its establishment - the highest level of football in Gibraltar. The league was contested by ten clubs, expanded from eight clubs in the last two seasons, including all clubs from [last season](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Gibraltar_Premier_Division "2014–15 Gibraltar Premier Division") and two promoted clubs from 2014–15 Gibraltar Second Division.[[1]](#cite_note-ten_teams-1) The winner of the Gibraltar Premier Division, [Lincoln Red Imps](/wiki/Lincoln_Red_Imps_F.C. "Lincoln Red Imps F.C.") was allocated a spot in Champions League qualification, and as they won the Rock Cup a spot in the Europa League was given to the runners-up [Europa FC](/wiki/Europa_F.C. "Europa F.C.").[[2]](#cite_note-rules-2)
Club changed name from College Europa FC to Europa FC starting this season

[Lincoln Red Imps](/wiki/Lincoln_Red_Imps_F.C. "Lincoln Red Imps F.C.") qualified for the [Europa League first qualifying round](/wiki/2016%E2%80%9317_UEFA_Europa_League#First_qualifying_round "2016–17 UEFA Europa League") by winning the [2016 Rock Cup](/wiki/2016_Rock_Cup "2016 Rock Cup"). However, since they already qualified for European competition based on their league position, the spot awarded to the cup winners was passed down the league.

The **2015–16 [Gibraltar Premier Division](/wiki/Gibraltar_Premier_Division "Gibraltar Premier Division")** (known as the **Argus Insurance Premier Division** for sponsorship reasons) was the 117th season of the national [amateur](/wiki/Amateur "Amateur") and [semi-professional](/wiki/Semi-professional "Semi-professional") [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") league in [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar") since its establishment - the highest level of football in Gibraltar. The league was contested by ten clubs, expanded from eight clubs in the last two seasons, including all clubs from [last season](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Gibraltar_Premier_Division "2014–15 Gibraltar Premier Division") and two promoted clubs from 2014–15 Gibraltar Second Division.[[1]](#cite_note-ten_teams-1) The winner of the Gibraltar Premier Division, [Lincoln Red Imps](/wiki/Lincoln_Red_Imps_F.C. "Lincoln Red Imps F.C.") was allocated a spot in Champions League qualification, and as they won the Rock Cup a spot in the Europa League was given to the runners-up [Europa FC](/wiki/Europa_F.C. "Europa F.C.").[[2]](#cite_note-rules-2)

[Lincoln Red Imps](/wiki/Lincoln_Red_Imps_F.C. "Lincoln Red Imps F.C.") were the reigning champions, sealing a record 21st title [last season](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Gibraltar_Premier_Division "2014–15 Gibraltar Premier Division"). The Gibraltar football season kicked off with the Pepe Reyes Cup between Lincoln and Europa FC, which Lincoln won 3–2 after extra time. The season proper kicked off on the following Friday, September 25, with St Joseph's facing Lions.

## Format

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015%E2%80%9316_Gibraltar_Premier_Division&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Format")]

Each of the ten Premier Division teams will play each other three times for a total of 27 matches each.[[3]](#cite_note-fixtures-3) It is expected that the tenth-placed team will be relegated while the ninth-placed team from the Premier Division will enter a playoff with the second-placed team from the Second Division for a spot in the 2016–17 Premier Division.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Promotion/relegation play-off

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015%E2%80%9316_Gibraltar_Premier_Division&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Promotion/relegation play-off")]

At the end of the season, the ninth-placed team from the Premier Division, [Britannia XI](/wiki/FC_Britannia_XI "FC Britannia XI"), entered a play-off with [Mons Calpe](/wiki/Mons_Calpe_S.C. "Mons Calpe S.C."), the second-placed team from the Second Division for a spot in the 2016–17 Premier Division. [[2]](#cite_note-rules-2)

31 May 2016 (2016-05-31)

20:30 [CEST](/wiki/Central_European_Summer_Time "Central European Summer Time")

| [Britannia XI](/wiki/FC_Britannia_XI "FC Britannia XI") | 1–2 | [Mons Calpe](/wiki/Mons_Calpe_S.C. "Mons Calpe S.C.") |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Gilroy  2' | [Report](https://web.archive.org/web/20160624205419/http://www.gibraltarfa.com/domestic-football/match/184750) | V. Gonzalez  50', 52' |

[Victoria Stadium](/wiki/Victoria_Stadium_(Gibraltar) "Victoria Stadium (Gibraltar)"), [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar")

*Mons Calpe are promoted to the [2016–17 Gibraltar Premier Division](/wiki/2016%E2%80%9317_Gibraltar_Premier_Division "2016–17 Gibraltar Premier Division").*
